title: adversarial-review.sh claude-opus-4-7 empty-content at >40K-input scale
pre_sprint_4_behavior: claude-opus-4-7 (reasoning-class) emits empty content at >40K-input scale. Pre-Sprint-4 cheval had no streaming-with-recovery guard; the stream completed with empty .content and the per-call envelope passed validation as 'clean'. Adversarial review then produced no findings despite reasoning-class engagement.
post_sprint_4_behavior: Two-layer defense: (1) pre-flight gate routes through chunking when estimated_input > ceiling × 0.7 (35000 × 0.7 = 24500; 40000 > 24500 → chunk). (2) streaming-with-recovery on the dispatched chunks: empty_content_window_tokens=200 aborts the stream if first 200 tokens are empty; cot_budget_tokens=500 aborts if reasoning-class CoT runs away without visible content. Either path produces typed EmptyContent / cot_budget_exhausted exit, which the chain-walk consumes for fallback.
